exact_statement.name: R1c maximal-separation long-chord exclusion
exact_statement.formal_surface: For every repository-native admissible finite-patch FGL instance I, every trivial witness boundary w in W^triv(I), and every long-chord edge e_i with i in {1,2}, if e_i realizes a maximal endpoint separation D_i and every trivial witness boundary support is contained in a local patch of diameter strictly less than D_i, then e_i is not in supp(w).
quantifiers: forall long_chord_edge e_i with i in {1,2}
hypotheses: if e_i is in supp(w), then support_diameter(w) >= D_i
exact_statement.geometric_core: A support containing an edge whose endpoints are separated by D_i has diameter at least D_i; a support contained in a local patch of diameter strictly less than D_i cannot contain that edge.
exact_statement.r1_dependency_role: This discharges the maximal-separation contradiction pattern needed by R1c once the repository supplies the local-patch diameter bound and the support-diameter bridge for trivial witness boundaries.

proof_certificate.support_edge_diameter_bridge: e_i in supp(w) implies D_i <= support_diameter(w) <= delta_w
derivation: Assume for contradiction that e_i is in supp(w).
By the support-edge diameter bridge, support_diameter(w) is at least D_i.
By local containment, support_diameter(w) is at most delta_w.
By the maximal-separation local-patch bound, delta_w < D_i.
Therefore D_i <= delta_w < D_i, impossible in a strict order.
Hence e_i is not in supp(w).

discharged_subclaim.name: R1c geometric exclusion from maximal-separation diameter bound
discharged_subclaim.logical_form: If every trivial witness boundary is contained in a local patch whose diameter is strictly smaller than the long-chord endpoint separation, then no such boundary can contain the long-chord edge.
conditional_on: repository-native support-diameter bridge: containing e_i forces support diameter at least EndpointSeparation(e_i)
repository-native local-patch containment: trivial witness boundary support diameter is bounded by its local patch diameter
repository-native maximal-separation estimate: local patch diameter bound is strictly less than each long-chord endpoint separation

remaining_for_r1.r1a: OPEN unless separately proved: every trivial 2-face boundary excludes both long-chord edges.
remaining_for_r1.r1b: CONDITIONAL algebraic reduction only: F2 linear combinations preserve absence once generator absence is supplied.
remaining_for_r1.r1c_inputs: OPEN unless already present in the repo: support-diameter bridge, local-patch containment, and strict maximal-separation diameter estimate.
remaining_for_r1.generation_bridge: OPEN generation bridge unless already present in the repo: W^triv is exactly the F2 span of the trivial 2-face boundaries used by R1a/R1b.

required_non_claims: does not prove R1c without the diameter inputs
does not prove combined R1 Long-Chord Exclusion Lemma
does not prove P vs NP
does not prove any Clay problem
